regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ Pair — as of 2026-06-12 12:28 UTC. CAVEAT UP FRONT: this briefing is intelligence, not a validated edge. My own scorecard is n=3 directional calls (hit 1.0) and regime accuracy 0.286 — that's CALIBRATING, statistically meaningless until ~30-60 samples. Treat everything below as a structured read, not a guarantee. And critically: CPI prints at 8:30 ET TODAY — that single event can override every structural argument here.

1) MOVE-BY-MOVE READ. Both indices are constructively positioned but not stretched. ES last 7432.5, up ~0.49% on the day and ~0.43% on the week; NQ last 29579.5, up ~0.39% on the day and a stronger ~1.91% on the week. The weekly divergence — NQ +1.9% vs ES +0.4% — tells you tech carried the bounce while the broad tape lagged, consistent with the 5-day spread (+0.30%) favoring NQ even as the 20-day spread is essentially flat (+0.05%). Both trade ABOVE prior-day highs (ES 7420, NQ 29544) and above their session VWAPs (ES 7419, NQ 29503), so the overnight/pre-open auction is leaning bid. RSI is neutral-to-soft (ES 45.9, NQ 50.2) — no momentum exhaustion, plenty of room either way. Likely catalysts: the dominant news theme is GEOPOLITICS/ENERGY — escalating Trump-Iran rhetoric (Kharg Island, Hormuz oil, 'bomb' threats) plus an ECB rate HIKE explicitly tied to Iran-war energy costs. That's a stagflationary-tilt overhang (higher energy → sticky inflation → central-bank hawkishness) that caps upside conviction and raises CPI-day tail risk. Both indices holding above prior-day highs INTO that backdrop is mildly impressive, but it's fragile.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ (where volume traded — NOT order-flow delta; no buy/sell aggression info here). ES: POC 7395.57, value area 7290.91–7450.97, HVNs clustered 7395/7414/7445 above and 7297/7321 below. Price at 7432.5 sits in the upper third of the value area, just under the 7445 HVN and the 7451 VAH — that band is the immediate ceiling. The line in the sand is the POC at ~7396; losing it puts 7321 and 7297 (the lower accumulation shelf) in play. NQ: POC 29457.43, value area 28625.51–29684.32, HVNs 29457/29192 below and a thin shelf up toward 29684. NQ at 29579.5 is above its POC and pressing toward the VAH at 29684 — that's the upside magnet/resistance. The accumulation floor sits at the 29457 POC, then a large air pocket down to 28890/28738. NQ's line in the sand is the POC ~29457: above it, the upper-VA grind stays intact; a clean break below opens fast-fill territory toward the high-28k HVNs.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME. Regime = BREADTH. Contemporaneous correlation 0.941, lag-0 cross-correlation 0.966, no meaningful leader (best_lag -2 at -0.037 is noise), divergence FALSE. ES and NQ are moving as one body — broad agreement means the directional signal is more DURABLE and more trustworthy than in a concentration regime where a handful of mega-caps drag NQ around. Implication: you can size a touch more confidently on DIRECTION when it appears, because both legs confirm. Note the regime label is an INPUT, not an edge, and there's no execution path for these CME futures in this stack (research only).

4) EVENT CALENDAR. CPI is TODAY, 8:30 ET (cadence estimate, but treat as live) — this dominates. Reduce size into the window; do not carry conviction through the print. FOMC in 5 days (June 17, seed) — widen stops / trim size into that window too. PCE ~June 30, ISM ~July 1, NFP ~July 3 are further out (cadence estimates, approximate). The near-term calendar is HEAVY: CPI today + FOMC in a week, layered on Iran/energy headline risk. That argues for smaller size and faster hands regardless of bias.

5) SYNTHESIS — META-REGIME + NARROWED BIAS. Meta-regime: a BREADTH-confirmed, modestly constructive tape (both indices above prior-day highs and VWAP, well above the 200-SMA — ES +7.6%, NQ +14.6%) operating UNDER a stagflationary geopolitical cloud and INTO a CPI print. The structural picture (clean correlation, holding above value, NQ leading the week) tilts mildly LONG. But the news is NOT clean — energy/Iran escalation plus an ECB hike is a genuine two-sided risk — and CPI can flip everything in one candle. So per my own rules, confidence stays capped. NARROWED BIAS: mild long-the-trend / buy-pullbacks-into-value bias, ~55% (degraded to an effective ~48% blended confidence given CPI tail risk and an unproven scorecard). Preferred long zone is pullbacks into the NQ POC ~29457 (and ES POC ~7396) where accumulation sits. INVALIDATION: bias is invalidated on a sustained break and acceptance below NQ 29457 (POC) — below there the air pocket toward 28890/28738 opens and the BREADTH signal flips bearish since ES would simultaneously be losing its own 7396 POC. NEUTRALIZE into 8:30 ET CPI regardless of this read.
